criteria.description: A net-new module under src/dashboard/ exports a pure, import-safe function that, given an explicit run-folder path (and a caller-supplied 'now'), reads state.json and trace.jsonl from that folder and returns a plain JS object of display-mapped fields. Importing the module and calling the assembler starts no server, binds no port, and performs no filesystem writes.
criteria.verifyBy: Vitest imports the module and calls the assembler on the complete-run fixture; test asserts a plain object is returned. Grep the module source to confirm it imports only Node stdlib modules (e.g. node:fs / node:path, and benign helpers like node:url / node:process are permitted) — no http/server modules and no harness entrypoint that would drag the Anthropic SDK into the import closure.

criteria.description: The assembler returns an object with these exact, stable keys: runId, goal, currentSprint (number index), currentSprintTitle (string), contractVersion (number, the round), phase (string|null, current step), status, haltReason, contractFreezeReason, budgetSpentUsd, startedAt, elapsedMs (number|null), scores (per-sprint scores, shape pinned in c3), and degraded (boolean). On the complete-run fixture these map to: runId=state.runId, goal=state.goal, currentSprint=state.currentSprint, currentSprintTitle=state.sprints[state.currentSprint].title, contractVersion=state.contractVersion, phase=phase of the LAST trace.jsonl line (regardless of that line's phase type), status=state.status, haltReason=state.haltReason, contractFreezeReason=state.contractFreezeReason, budgetSpentUsd=state.budgetSpentUsd, startedAt=state.startedAt, and degraded=false. The complete fixture's LAST trace line is a non-EVALUATE phase (e.g. DECIDE) so 'phase = last line, any phase' is genuinely proven and not coincidentally satisfied by an EVALUATE last line.
criteria.verifyBy: Vitest asserts each enumerated key is present and deep-equals the corresponding value from the fixture's state.json / last trace line, including expect(result.runId).toBe(fixtureState.runId); expect(result.startedAt).toBe(fixtureState.startedAt); expect(result.goal).toBe(fixtureState.goal); expect(result.currentSprint).toBe(fixtureState.currentSprint); expect(result.currentSprintTitle).toBe(fixtureState.sprints[fixtureState.currentSprint].title); expect(result.phase).toBe(lastTraceLine.phase); test additionally asserts lastTraceLine.phase !== 'EVALUATE' to confirm the last-line-wins rule; expect(result.degraded).toBe(false).

criteria.description: result.scores is an array of { sprint: number, score: number } objects derived from EVALUATE trace events in trace-file order (one entry per EVALUATE line, carrying that line's own sprint and score fields). When trace.jsonl contains no EVALUATE events (or is absent), result.scores falls back to state.scores[] preserving its real semantics as a flat append-log: each numeric entry maps to { sprint: null, score: state.scores[i] } (index i is NOT treated as a sprint number, since state.scores is a flat run-wide log).
criteria.verifyBy: Vitest on the complete fixture (which has EVALUATE lines) asserts result.scores deep-equals the ordered [{sprint, score}, …] built by reading each EVALUATE line's sprint and score. A dedicated fixture/case with a populated state.scores[] and ZERO EVALUATE lines asserts result.scores deep-equals state.scores.map((score) => ({ sprint: null, score })), confirming the fallback branch is exercised and no false per-sprint index is fabricated.

criteria.description: result.elapsedMs is computed as caller-supplied 'now' minus Date.parse(state.startedAt) (not from Date.now() internally), yielding a deterministic duration in milliseconds when startedAt is present and parseable. When state.startedAt is absent or unparseable (Date.parse yields NaN), result.elapsedMs is null (never NaN).
criteria.verifyBy: Vitest passes a fixed 'now' on the complete fixture and asserts result.elapsedMs === now − Date.parse(fixtureState.startedAt); a second call with a different 'now' yields the correspondingly different value. On the missing-fields/corrupt cases (absent or unparseable startedAt) asserts expect(result.elapsedMs).toBeNull().

criteria.description: An exported most-recently-modified auto-discovery helper, given a runs/ directory path, returns the newest (highest mtime) run folder path when no explicit run path is supplied; given an empty or nonexistent runs/ directory it returns null (does not throw).
criteria.verifyBy: Vitest builds a temp runs/ dir with two run folders of differing mtimes and asserts the discovery function returns the most-recently-modified one; a second case on an empty temp dir and a nonexistent path each asserts it returns null and does not throw.

criteria.description: Graceful degradation without throwing across the degradation fixtures/cases, always returning a non-null plain object: (a) missing-fields — the named optional fields budgetSpentUsd and runDir, when absent from a PARSEABLE state.json, surface as null in the returned object, and degraded === false (absent optional fields do NOT flip the degraded discriminator); (b) mid-run partial (status 'running', fewer scores than sprints) — assembles the present fields with status preserved, and degraded === false (a parseable-but-incomplete state is not corrupt); (c) corrupt/half-written state.json that does not parse — the assembler catches the parse failure and returns a non-null plain object with degraded === true (the explicit 'updating' / last-known-good discriminator) rather than throwing; degraded is reserved SOLELY for this unparseable case, not for merely-absent optional fields; (d) empty or absent trace.jsonl — does not throw and yields phase === null.
criteria.verifyBy: Vitest wraps each case in expect(() => assemble(...)).not.toThrow(). Missing-fields: expect(result.budgetSpentUsd).toBeNull(); expect(result.runDir).toBeNull(); expect(result.degraded).toBe(false). Mid-run partial: expect(result.status).toBe('running'); expect(result.degraded).toBe(false); present fields mapped. Corrupt: expect(result).not.toBeNull(); expect(typeof result).toBe('object'); expect(result.degraded).toBe(true). Empty/absent trace: expect(result.phase).toBeNull() and no throw.

criteria.description: Checked-in fixture run directories exist in the test tree covering: (a) complete finished run WITH EVALUATE trace lines whose LAST trace line is a non-EVALUATE phase (e.g. DECIDE); (b) mid-run partial (status running, fewer scores than sprints); (c) missing-fields (budgetSpentUsd/runDir absent, state.json still parseable); (d) corrupt/half-written state.json that does not parse; (e) empty/absent trace.jsonl coverage (via a fixture or an in-test temp dir); and (f) a fixture (or in-test temp dir) with a populated state.scores[] and ZERO EVALUATE lines so c3's fallback branch is exercised. Each contains the state.json (and trace.jsonl where applicable) the tests read.
criteria.verifyBy: Fixture directories/files are present on disk under the test tree and are the inputs the c2–c6 tests load; test run confirms they are readable/parsed as intended, including the non-EVALUATE last-line in (a) and the zero-EVALUATE-with-state.scores case in (f).

criteria.description: The whole vitest suite passes offline with the new tests added, and no new runtime dependency is introduced (package.json dependencies unchanged; module uses Node stdlib only).
criteria.verifyBy: `npm test` passes with the baseline suite plus the new tests green; diff of package.json shows no additions to dependencies.

scope.description: Confine changes to a net-new src/dashboard/ module, its test file(s) under tests/, and checked-in fixture run directories in the test tree. Do not refactor unrelated harness modules.
Pure reader only: never write, create, or modify any file in a run folder, and never read or write config. No HTTP server, no page rendering in this sprint (deferred to a later sprint).
Do not modify the harness's progress emission (trace writer, orchestrator, state schema). Read only the existing on-disk format.
No new dependencies: Node built-in modules only (fs/path); nothing added to package.json dependencies. Follow repo conventions — tests in tests/, ES-module .js import specifiers.
